meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken API integrations. Learn a professional workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug and ship faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing silent failures. The key to fixing broken API integrations is isolating the handshake protocol and mapping response schemas against your local state. By using Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based debugging, you can identify and resolve integration bottlenecks in minutes, not hours.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client is expecting a live dashboard, but the third-party API is returning 422 errors, and your console is a graveyard of unhelpful stack traces. You’ve spent three hours refactoring, but the bug remains elusive, turning a profitable project into a resource-draining nightmare.

This isn't a failure of your ability; it’s a failure of your tooling. As freelancers, we don't have the luxury of infinite debugging sessions. We need a systematic, repeatable approach to API orchestration that turns black-box errors into transparent, actionable data. It’s time to stop guessing and start engineering with precision.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before touching code, use Antigravity to map the expected schema vs the actual response to identify structural mismatches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided API documentation and my local interface definition. Identify any discrepancies in data types, nested object structures, or required header fields that would trigger a 422 or 400 error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./api/schema.ts ./src/types/interface.ts --query 'compare these for structural drift'

steps.title: Isolate the Handshake
steps.description: Capture the raw request/response cycle to see exactly what the server sees.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust logging wrapper for this Axios instance that intercepts requests and responses, sanitizes sensitive keys, and logs the full payload to a local debug file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write ./src/utils/apiInterceptor.ts --task 'implement axios interceptor for logging'

steps.title: Automated Error Reproduction
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write a minimal reproduction test case that hits the failing endpoint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that mocks this API integration, specifically testing the edge case where the server returns a 422 status code.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Parsing
steps.description: Wrap your API responses in a Zod schema to fail loudly and early when data is malformed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this API service to use Zod for runtime validation. If the response doesn't match the schema, throw a descriptive error indicating exactly which field failed validation.

steps.title: Final Integration Verification
steps.description: Run a full integration check to ensure no regression was introduced during the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire codebase for any lingering references to the old, broken API implementation and ensure all calls are now routed through the new validated service.

pro_tips: Always log the 'X-Request-ID' header to correlate your local logs with the provider's server-side logs.
Use a local proxy like 'mitmproxy' to inspect outgoing traffic if the API is encrypted and you cannot log it directly.
Treat every API integration as a third-party dependency; never trust the incoming data types without a runtime validation layer.
When debugging, force the API to return a 500 error intentionally to ensure your error-handling UI doesn't crash the entire application.
Document your API 'quirks' in a dedicated README.md file—clients pay extra for the institutional knowledge you gain during the build.
content.next_steps: Once your integration is stable, implement a circuit breaker pattern to ensure your app stays responsive even if the third-party API goes down.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting buggy APIs dictate your delivery timeline. Master the tools that turn complex integrations into routine tasks. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
